steps.title: Identify slope-intercept form parameters
steps.content:
For a linear function, we use the slope-intercept form: $f(x) = mx + b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept. From the problem:
  • Slope = $\frac{3}{4}$, so $m = \frac{3}{4}$
  • $f(0) = 3$ means when $x = 0$, the output is 3. This is the y-intercept, so $b = 3$

steps.title: Write the equation and match the choice
steps.content: Substitute $m = \frac{3}{4}$ and $b = 3$ into the slope-intercept form: $$f(x) = \frac{3}{4}x + 3$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
